sutta: [Music] the buddha said monks there are these 5 states not to be got 2 by recluse or brahmin by deva mara or brahma nor by anyone in the world what 5 where ageing brings no old age that state is not to be got 2 by recluse or brahmin etc where sickening brings no sickness nor dying death nor wasting destruction nor ending brings the end that state is not to be got to by recluse of brahmin by deva mara or brahma nor by anyone in the world monks to the unlearned average man aging brings old age and when he is old he reflects not thus not to me only does aging bring old age but wheresoever there is a coming and going a passing on and arising of creatures to all aging brings old age and if when old age comes i should mourn and find weep and wail and beat the breasts and fall into destruction enemies would rejoice while friends would grieve food would not please me ugliness would come upon my body affairs would be neglected and enemies would rejoice while friends would weep would grieve and when old age comes he mourns spines weeps whales beats his breasts and falls into destruction amongst this man is called an unlearned average man pierced by the poison dart of sorrow he just torments himself again monks to the unlearned average man sickening brings sickness dying death wasting destruction and ending brings the end and when the end is near he reflects not thus not to me only does ending bring the end but wheres wherever there is a coming and growing of creatures passing on and arising to all ending brings the end and if when the end is near i should mourn and find weep and wheel and beat the breast and fall into destruction enemies would rejoice while friends would grieve food would not please me ugliness would come would come upon my body affairs would be neglected and enemies would rejoice while friends would weep and when the end is near he moans spines weeps wheels beats his breast and falls into destruction monks this man is called an unlearned average man pierced by this poison dan of sorrow he just torments himself to the learned aryan disciple also amongst 18 brings old age but when he is old he does reflect in that forced way that not to me only does ending bring the end but wheresoever there is a coming going of creatures passing on etc etc and when age comes he does not mourn nor pine nor weep nor will nor beat his breast nor fall into destruction monks this man is called a learned aryan disciple drawn out is the poisoned dawn of sorrow with which the unlearned average man torments himself the sorrowless dart-free aryan disciple has cooled the self entirely so also to the learned aryan disciple sickening brings sickness dying death wasting destruction ending the end and when the end is near it reflects in a light manner you know that is not only to me does ending bring the end but wheres wherever there is a coming going of creatures etc etc monks this man is called a learned aryan disciple drawn out is the poison dart of sorrow with which the unlearned average man torments himself the sorrowless dark free aryan disciple has cooled the self entirely monks these are the 5 states not to be god too by recluse or brahmin by deva mara or brahma or by anyone in the world is the end of the sutta
commentary: here basically saying that no being can avoid aging sickness and dying although the form it takes may vary for example in the heavenly world it is not so visible you don't see the aging so visible and only person does not reflect properly when he's old or when he's dying that all beings must grow old and die and so he does not accept aging and tries to make himself for example look younger he does not accept death and looks for all ways to cure himself but 1 who understands the dharma accepts aging he grows old gracefully when death comes he knows he's lived his life skillfully and he's not remorseful and because he knows he's bound for a good rebirth so that's about uh the suta the last thing i have to say is a lot of people when they are sick for example with cancer etc they rush here and they're trying to look for a cure because they don't accept death but if they find a cure they are very very happy but then they forget that cure is only for a short while and death will come again to visit them so but the person who understands the dhamma he accepts all these things growing old sickening dying so when death comes and he accepts it gracefully as you saw the other day and this lady who came from ippo despite of being so sick she came so determined to come and after coming for that rope offering the next few days she was uh she's been like ac she's she's been in extremely high spirits very good last spirits now we come to suta number

items: where ageing brings no old age
where dying death brings no death
where wasting destruction brings no destruction
